symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic intermittent foul-smelling, blood-stained purulent discharge from a perianal skin opening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discharging perianal opening is the hallmark external feature of a fistula-in-ano.
Haemorrhoids prolapse mucosa and bleed but do not produce a discrete discharging skin opening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute reduction in discharge with rising pain, swelling and erythema over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Falling drainage with rising pressure symptoms is the classic picture of a tract that has blocked and is re-forming a collection.
Escalating pain and swelling is the presentation that must be examined to exclude early necrotising infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Passage of flatus through the external perianal opening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gas exiting the external opening demonstrates a communication between the bowel lumen and the perianal skin — a track, i.e. a fistula.
A large proportion of perianal fistulae arise as the sequel of a previous perianal abscess.
A low-grade temperature is consistent with a localised soft-tissue infection; a high fever with rigors would signal systemic sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable indurated fibrous cord running from the external opening toward the anal canal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable cord is the physical signature of the fibrous fistula track — a clinician-only finding, sealed; the AI cannot palpate it via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: External opening 2 cm from the anal verge at the 4 o'clock position with purulent exudate expressible on gentle pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defining the external opening and expressing pus localises the track; requires perineal inspection and palpation — sealed clinician finding.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Perianal abscess requiring incision and drainage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presence of fluctuance changes management to urgent drainage; its absence cannot be confirmed via telehealth, which is why an in-person exam is required.

investigation_details.expected_result: Defines the primary track, secondary extensions and relationship to the sphincter complex
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Surgery cannot be planned without track mapping; not a telehealth-obtainable result.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: MRI is the reference standard for mapping the track prior to surgery — a hospital/outpatient investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.
A raised white cell count and CRP gauge the systemic burden of infection — venous bloods, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Anal fistula with acute perianal abscess
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic discharging track (with flatus through the opening) on a background of prior abscess, now with falling drainage and rising pain/swelling — a re-forming, possibly obstructed collection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person perineal examination and digital rectal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important next step — defines the external/internal openings, assesses for fluctuance/a drainable collection and excludes features of necrotising infection. Cannot be done via telehealth.
Gauge the systemic inflammatory burden and support the decision on oral versus IV antibiotics and hospital referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: General-surgical / colorectal referral for examination under anaesthesia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive assessment and management of the fistula (fistulotomy or seton) and drainage of any abscess.
Maps the track prior to definitive surgery; arranged through the surgical pathway, not acutely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Screening for underlying Crohn disease (bowel symptom review, specialist assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recurrent perianal fistulising disease can be the presenting feature of Crohn disease.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate CT/urgent surgical review for suspected necrotising infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated on the current localised, systemically-stable picture; reserved for the emergency pathway if red-flag features develop (do not over-investigate/over-escalate the stable presentation).
